top10.introHtml: Ranked for a cheap fun second car — top condition comes first, then model reliability, then value. Refreshed 2026-05-12 from Cars.com (24 listings), CarGurus (111 total), and AutoTrader (97 total). TrueCar URL filter returned SUVs this run — being investigated for next refresh.

picks.title: 2006 Mazda MX-5 Miata Touring
picks.whyGood: The Miata is the definitive budget roadster — featherweight, balanced, bulletproof. 82K miles is young for an NC-generation (2006–2015) Miata, which routinely runs 200K+ with basic maintenance. Touring trim adds Bilstein shocks, leather, and a heated windscreen. No-accident history is the cherry on top. At $10,995 this is priced right for a clean example.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top through a full open/close cycle — listen for motor hesitation and check the rear plastic window for yellowing or crazing. Inspect the fabric top seam at the rear quarter for separating stitching. Check rocker panels for rust (common on Miatas in the mid-Atlantic). The 2006 NC uses a timing chain — no belt interval needed. Ask for service records on the clutch.
picks.bottomLine: Best buy in the list — fun, fixable, appreciating, and already de-risked by the no-accident report.

picks.title: 2001 BMW 325 Ci
picks.whyGood: The E46 3-series convertible is one of the best-looking, best-driving BMW convertibles ever made. 60,200 miles on a 2001 is remarkably low — this car has barely been driven. Cars.com rates it a Great Deal. The 2.5L M54 inline-6 is smooth, eager, and parts are plentiful. Nearby at 16 miles.
picks.whatToVerify: E46 cooling systems are the famous weak point — inspect for cracked plastic coolant expansion tank, leaking water pump, and radiator condition. Check VANOS (variable valve timing) — rough idle at startup suggests wear. Operate the top through a full cycle; check the hydraulic rams under the rear trim for leaks. Inspect for rust at trunk lip and underneath rear subframe mounting points. Pull codes before buying.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mile E46 3-series convertibles at $10K are genuinely rare — move fast.

picks.title: 1994 Ford Mustang GT Convertible
picks.location: Private seller Joseph, Eddington, PA (29 mi)
picks.whyGood: 44,000 miles on a 1994 Mustang GT is extraordinary — this car has barely turned over. SN95 Mustang GT convertible with a pushrod 5.0L V8, simple mechanicals, massive aftermarket support, and an authentic V8 rumble at low money. Private seller often means genuine history and motivated pricing.
picks.whatToVerify: Check the convertible top frame for rust — SN95 frames rust at the hinges and bow joints, causing the top to fold unevenly. Inspect the fabric top for UV damage. Check floor pans and trunk for water intrusion. Rear axle: look for clunking from U-joints or axle bearings. Verify the odometer reading is credible for such low mileage — request maintenance records and consider a Carfax pull.
picks.bottomLine: If the top frame and odometer check out, this is a unicorn — a rust-belt Mustang GT convertible with original-spec mileage.

picks.title: 2009 Mercedes-Benz CLK-Class CLK 350
picks.location: Elite Auto House, Philadelphia, PA (24 mi)
picks.whyGood: The CLK 350 is the V6 version of the CLK cabriolet — not the high-strung V8 that demands expensive maintenance, just a silky 3.5L naturally-aspirated engine in a gorgeous cabriolet body. 82,812 miles is well within range. Good Deal badge. The CLK 350 is significantly more reliable long-term than the CLK 500/55 AMG variants.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top fully — CLK cabriolets have a three-layer top with a glass rear window; check the window defroster for function. Inspect the hydraulic top motor (located in trunk) for fluid leaks. CLK common issues: front lower control arm bushings, battery drain from electronics. Check for rust at lower rocker panels and door sills. Verify service history for transmission fluid.
picks.bottomLine: Best value Mercedes in this batch — the V6 CLK 350 hits the sweet spot of luxury feel and reasonable upkeep cost.

picks.title: 2000 BMW Z3 2.3 Roadster
picks.location: Selden Motors, Willow Grove, PA (23 mi)
picks.whyGood: The Z3 is a pure roadster experience — two seats, inline-6, rear-wheel drive, and a fabric top. The 2.3L M52TU engine (1998–2000) is a sweet spot upgrade over the earlier 4-cylinder. 75,828 miles is reasonable. Selden Motors is a known local dealer. Good Deal badge.
picks.whatToVerify: Cooling system is the same concern as E46 — check expansion tank, water pump (plastic impeller failure), and hoses. Z3 tops are simple manual operations — check the top frame pivots for rust and the vinyl window for clarity. Look for rust at the rear crossmember behind the seats (a Z3-specific rust trap). Check for oil leaks from valve cover gasket and rear main seal.
picks.bottomLine: Z3 roadster life at $10,600 is a solid buy — simpler than a Z4 and arguably more characterful.

picks.title: 2005 Audi A4 3.0 Cabriolet quattro
picks.whyGood: The B6 Audi A4 Cabriolet with the 3.0L V6 and quattro AWD is the premium pick — all-weather capability in a convertible is rare and genuinely useful in PA. 80,101 miles and a Great Deal badge. The 3.0L is more reliable than the 1.8T in this generation. Quattro adds confidence in spring and fall top-down weather.
picks.whatToVerify: Audi cabriolet tops are electrically operated with a hydraulic pump — operate the top 5 full cycles and listen for hesitation. Check the rear window seam where the fabric meets the glass for separation (water ingress is the main enemy). Common B6 issues: timing chain tensioner, PCV valve rattle, oil consumption. Check for musty smell in cabin indicating past water leak.
picks.bottomLine: AWD convertible under $9K with Great Deal badge is unusual — the 3.0L quattro combination makes this genuinely versatile.

picks.title: 2007 Mercedes-Benz SLK-Class
picks.location: Broadway Auto Sales, Westville, NJ (16 mi)
picks.whyGood: The R171 SLK retractable hardtop is a mechanical party trick that holds up well — hard roof folds into the trunk in 22 seconds. Broadway Auto Sales has a 4.4 dealer rating. Great Deal badge plus High Demand signals this is moving. The hardtop eliminates all soft-top concerns. At 16 miles away, it's convenient to visit.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the retractable roof 3+ times and watch for hesitation or error lights. Electrical gremlins (window regulators, convertible top control module) are the known R171 weak points at higher mileage — 95K is approaching the threshold. Verify no oil leaks from the 3.5L V6. Ask for brake system service history and check for any top-related warning lights.
picks.bottomLine: The hardtop is a genuine differentiator, but budget for one electrical repair in the first year — it's part of SLK ownership at this mileage.

picks.title: 2012 MINI Cooper S Convertible
picks.whyGood: The R57 MINI Cooper S convertible with the turbo 1.6L N18 engine is quick, sharp, and genuinely fun. 98K miles and a clean accident history. The go-kart handling reputation is well-earned. At $9,400 with no accidents it's priced right.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the MINI's electric folding top — check for smooth operation and listen for the motor straining. R57 common issues: water pump and thermostat failure, carbon buildup on intake valves, timing chain tensioner noise on cold start. Check the soft top for tears at the rear corners. MINI tops develop leaks at the weatherstrip around the windshield header — press the headliner to feel for moisture.
picks.bottomLine: Most fun-per-dollar of the sub-$10K picks — buy it, enjoy it, budget for the water pump.

picks.title: 2017 MINI Convertible Cooper S
picks.location: Royal Auto Group, Haddon Township, NJ (18 mi)
picks.whyGood: The F57 generation (2016+) MINI convertible is a meaningfully better car than the R57 it replaced — stiffer structure, better electronics, improved reliability track record. 73,773 miles and a Great Deal badge. At $12,742 you get a nearly modern MINI Cooper S with CarPlay, updated styling, and the B48 turbo 4-cylinder. Royal Auto Group has a 3.5 dealer rating.
picks.whatToVerify: Electric soft top: operate multiple full cycles. F57 tops have an inner canvas plus outer vinyl — check both layers at the rear corners. B48 engine is generally solid; check for timing chain noise on cold start. Ask if CarPlay is activated. Check for any open recalls on the F57 for the folding top mechanism. Verify service records for brake fluid and differential fluid changes.
picks.bottomLine: The newest, most reliable, and most livable car on the list — worth the premium over the 2012 if you want fewer surprises.

picks.title: 1999 Porsche Boxster
picks.location: Divan Auto Group, Feasterville-Trevose, PA (27 mi)
picks.whyGood: A genuine Porsche roadster under $13K with only 56,871 miles is legitimately rare. The 986 Boxster is a mid-engine, rear-wheel-drive sports car with sublime balance and handling. Divan Auto Group has a 4.4 dealer rating. Good Deal badge. This is the car that punches hardest above its price in terms of pure driving experience.
picks.whatToVerify: IMS BEARING IS THE CRITICAL CHECK: The 1999 Boxster 986 uses an intermediate shaft bearing that can fail catastrophically, destroying the engine. Ask specifically whether the IMS bearing has been upgraded (LN Engineering retrofit or documented Porsche replacement). If no documentation exists, budget $1,500–$2,500 for the retrofit as a pre-purchase condition or walk away. Also check: RMS (rear main seal) for oil leak onto exhaust, soft top hydraulic cylinders for leaks, HVAC blower motor. A PPI from an independent Porsche specialist is strongly advised.
picks.bottomLine: The most rewarding drive on this list — but only buy it with documented IMS bearing service or a pre-purchase inspection from a Porsche specialist.
